name: Piedratajada and Marracos — circular from Valpalmas
name_original: Piedratajada y Marracos — circular desde Valpalmas
region_intro: Road cycling around Valpalmas offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains. The region features historical sites such as castles and monasteries, alongside natural elements like reservoirs. Road cyclists can expect varied terrain with moderate elevation changes, providing a mix of challenges and scenic stretches. Best road cycling routes around Valpalmas The most popular road cycling route is Piedratajada and Marracos — circular from Valpalmas, a 37.9 miles (61.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora - circular from Erla, a difficult 70.3 miles (113.2 km) path. This route includes significant climbs and passes through mountainous terrain and historical sites. Local road cyclists also love the Parish Church of Marracos – Sotonera Reservoir loop from Marracos, a 38.6 miles (62.2 km) trail leading through rural areas and past the Sotonera Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours. Road cycling around Valpalmas is defined by rolling hills, historical landmarks, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valpalmas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Valpalmas?
A: Road cycling around Valpalmas features a diverse landscape of rolling hills and agricultural plains. You'll encounter varied terrain with moderate elevation changes, offering a mix of challenging climbs and scenic, flatter stretches.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Valpalmas?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the area, several 'moderate' routes are suitable for those looking for a manageable ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993005198/piedratajada-and-marracos-circular-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada and Marracos — circular from Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 37.9-mile (61.1 km) loop that offers a good introduction to the local countryside.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near Valpalmas?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Valpalmas offers routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora - circular from Erla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 70.3-mile (113.2 km) path that includes substantial climbs through mountainous terrain and past historical sites. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – Luesia Castle loop from Balsa antigua&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 93 miles (149 km) with over 1500 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Valpalmas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993005198/piedratajada-and-marracos-circular-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada and Marracos — circular from Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Marracos – Sotonera Reservoir loop from Marracos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by historical sites and natural features. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3716853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yéquera Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Valpalmas?
A: Given the region's characteristics of rolling hills and agricultural plains, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Valpalmas. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be hot, while winters may bring cooler temperatures, though specific winter conditions can vary.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or offer water views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Marracos – Sotonera Reservoir loop from Marracos&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that leads through rural areas and directly past the Sotonera Reservoir, offering scenic water views.

Q: How long do road cycling routes near Valpalmas typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993005198/piedratajada-and-marracos-circular-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada and Marracos — circular from Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 45 minutes. More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor and return via the Monasterio de Monlora - circular from Erla&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Valpalmas?
A: Road cyclists using komoot often praise the varied terrain, which includes rolling hills and agricultural plains, as well as the presence of historical landmarks and scenic reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any less-traveled or 'hidden gem' routes for road cycling in Valpalmas?
A: While all routes offer unique experiences, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Monlora – Biel Castle loop from Alda de Monlora&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that combines historical interest with scenic views, potentially offering a quieter experience compared to the most popular trails. It covers 41.8 miles (67.2 km) and includes a visit to the Santuario de Monlora.

Q: What are the typical starting points or 'trailheads' for road cycling routes in Valpalmas?
A: Routes often start from various towns and villages within or near Valpalmas. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993005198/piedratajada-and-marracos-circular-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada and Marracos — circular from Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt; begins directly from Valpalmas. Other routes may start from nearby locations like Erla, Marracos, or Balsa antigua, as seen with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988484615/biel-through-the-puerto-de-sierra-mayor-and-return-via-the-monasterio-de-monlora-circular-from-erla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel through the Puerto de Sierra Mayor&lt;/a&gt; route from Erla.
tips: Known village for being near the spectacular Aguarales of Valpalmas and being the cradle of Santiago Ramón y Cajal where the birth house and museum of the Spanish doctor and scientist and Nobel Prize in Medicine is still preserved
If we pass through the town of Piedratajada, it is undoubtedly worth visiting the Church of San Sebastián, a spectacular Romanesque work!
Beautiful parish church of the town of Marracos in the region of Cinco Villas
